title: Performance Review Process and Self-Assessment Tips
text:
Q: What is the performance review process, timeline, and how can I effectively complete my self-assessment?
A: The performance review process occurs annually, typically starting in January and concluding by the end of March. Employees will receive a notification in early January to begin their self-assessment, which is due by January 31st. The self-assessment should reflect on your achievements, challenges, and areas for improvement over the past year. After submissions, managers will review self-assessments and schedule one-on-one meetings to discuss performance, usually in February. To prepare, consider using the STAR method (Situation, Task, Action, Result) to articulate your contributions clearly. Additionally, gather feedback from peers and reflect on your goals for the upcoming year. If you have questions or need assistance, please reach out to your HR representative by email at hr@company.com. Remember, this process is designed to support your professional growth and development.
